XOnlineStringLookupEx

Retrieves Unicode strings corresponding to an array of IDs for a given language.

HRESULT XOnlineStringLookupEx(
    DWORD dwTitleID,
    WORD wNumStringIDs,
    DWORD *pdwStringIDs,
    DWORD dwLanguage,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phTask
);

Parameters

dwTitleID
[in] Identifier of the title to query. This can be the current title ID or one of the valid alternate title IDs. If zero is specified, the current title ID is used.
wNumStringIDs
[in] Number of strings to verify.
pdwStringIDs
[in] Array of string identifiers for the strings to verify.
dwLanguage

[in] Value that specifies the language of the text to verify. This parameter must be one of the following values:
Value Description
XC_LANGUAGE_ENGLISH English
XC_LANGUAGE_JAPANESE Japanese
XC_LANGUAGE_GERMAN German
XC_LANGUAGE_FRENCH French
XC_LANGUAGE_SPANISH Spanish
XC_LANGUAGE_ITALIAN Italian
XC_LANGUAGE_KOREAN Korean
XC_LANGUAGE_TCHINESE Chinese (Traditional)
XC_LANGUAGE_PORTUGUESE Portuguese

To specify the language currently in use by the Xbox console (in other words, the language set by the user through the Dashboard), pass the return value of XGetLanguage as dwLanguage.

hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle to a caller-created event object. This object will be signaled when there is work to do by calling XOnlineTaskContinue. The caller may pass in NULL instead, indicating that the caller will poll XOnlineTaskContinue.
phTask
[out] Pointer to a variable of type XONLINETASK_HANDLE that receives a task handle representing the verify task. Pump this task to completion using XOnlineTaskContinue. When the task is complete, call XOnlineTaskClose to close the task.

Return Values

Returns S_OK when the lookup operation has started successfully.

XOnlineTaskContinue will return XONLINETASK_S_SUCCESS when the lookup operation has completed, at which time titles should call XOnlineStringLookupGetResults to retrieve the resulting strings.

Remarks

This function is identical to the XOnlineStringLookup function with the exception that an alternate title identifier may be specified. When looking up a string ID property from a message, pass the dwSenderTitleID from the message so the string lookup operates correctly for family title IDs.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineStringLookupGetResults, XOnlineStringVerify
